paragraphs.summary: This supervisory statement is addressed to Lloyd’s. It sets out the Prudential Regulation Authority’s (PRA’s) expectations in relation to the application of certain parts of Solvency II to Lloyd’s, an
In particular, this statement sets out the PRA’s expectations regarding the following topics:

This statement expands on the PRA’s general approach as set out in its insurance approach document.1 By clearly and consistently explaining its expectations of Lloyd’s in relation to the particular ar
This statement has been subject to public consultation2 and reflects the feedback that was received by the PRA.
Any reference to any provision of direct EU legislation is a reference to it as it forms part of retained EU law.

sections.chapter_title: Amendments to trust deeds, standard form letters of credit and guarantee arrangements

paragraphs.summary: Except in urgent cases, the PRA expects the Society to consult members, managing agents and other interested parties in the Lloyd’s market in relation to any proposed new Lloyd’s trust deed or amendme
The PRA would normally expect to receive the information required in Lloyd’s 6.3 for any new or amended Lloyd’s trust deed not less than three months in advance of the proposed change.
The PRA would also expect the Society to apply the approach in paragraphs 2.1 and 2.2 above in case of amendments to standard form letters of credit and guarantee arrangements.

The requirement to hold eligible own funds covering the central requirement is intended to ensure that risks to the Society, including risks to central assets (and in particular, the risk that own fun

Solvency Capital Requirement — General Provisions 6.6 recognises in its application to Lloyd’s that own funds attributable to a member are not available to absorb the losses of other members, or any l

The notional syndicate SCR is intended to facilitate the Society’s compliance with Solvency Capital Requirement — General Provisions 8.2. While the PRA expects the calculation of the notional SCR to m

In deriving the SCR, the Society should have regard to the notional SCR for each syndicate, that is calculated by managing agents either by reference to the standard formula or an internal model. Howe

The approach set out in Solvency Capital Requirement — Internal Models 17.2 is, when combined with the internal model requirements set out in Solvency Capital Requirement — Internal Models 10 to 16 an
Solvency Capital Requirement — General Provisions 7.3 requires the Society to calculate a central requi

As Lloyd’s is composed of syndicates which separately accept general insurance business or long-term insurance business, the PRA considers Lloyd’s to be a composite undertaking for the purposes of the
Composites 2.2 requires composite firms to maintain separate management of general insurance business and long-term insurance business. In pra

Paragraphs 2.1–2.3 of SS8/15 refer to a firm’s permission to effect and/or carry out contracts of insurance which may be general insurance business or long-term insurance business, or both, and, withi
The references in Composites 2.2 to ‘general insurance business’ and ‘long-term insurance business’, and the activities relating thereto, are to be similarly interpreted.
